sourceMovie.title: Minari
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Minari (2021) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Lee Isaac Chung's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Royal Tenenbaums
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Minari clicked for you, The Royal Tenenbaums (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Dances with Wolves
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Minari clicked for you, Dances with Wolves (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: What's Eating Gilbert Grape
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Minari clicked for you, What's Eating Gilbert Grape (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Minari?
faq.answer: Try The Royal Tenenbaums, Dances with Wolves, What's Eating Gilbert Grape—each shares DNA with Minari while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Minari?
faq.answer: Minari (2021) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Minari worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
